summary: Matched new character-select menu code in src/melee/mn/mncharsel.c, most notably mnCharSel_8026688C_OnEnter at 100%, and substantially improved mnCharSel_8025C020. The PR also moved large mncharsel static data into a new mncharsel.static.h, corrected symbol/data boundaries for mnCharSel data and strings, and tightened related gm/gmmain_lib/mn/sislib prototypes and struct field signedness needed by the match.
what_changed: Implemented mnCharSel_8026688C_OnEnter(void*) in mncharsel.c, including menu initialization, name-count scanning via mnName_8023754C, selected_since_load reset for four doors, audio setup, language-dependent archive loading for MnSlChr/MnExtAll, SIS setup, and final call to mnCharSel_802640A0.

Replaced the placeholder for mnCharSel_8025C020 with a large partial implementation handling character-select record/stat text and animations across match_type cases including 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, and 23.
Added small local helpers in mncharsel.c: inline1 for time formatting into HSD_Text, inline3 for finding/animating a JObj, and show_kos for Japanese versus non-Japanese KO text formatting.

Moved the large CSS icon and door static data from mncharsel.c into new src/melee/mn/mncharsel.static.h, preserving float macro constants for icon row heights and bounds because they cannot be enum values.
Changed mnCharSel_804D6CB0 declaration in the new static header to static CSSData* volatile, and retained static mnCharSel_804D6CF4.
Updated mncharsel.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures for mnCharSel_8025C020(int), mnCharSel_CursorThink(HSD_GObj*), and mnCharSel_8026688C_OnEnter(void*).
Expanded gm_1601.h with concrete return and argument types for many gm_80162xxx/gm_80163xxx accessors used by mnCharSel_8025C020, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for those entries.
Changed gmmain_lib accessors gmMainLib_8015D194, gmMainLib_8015D2BC, and gmMainLib_8015D3E4 from s8* to u8*, and changed struct gmm_x1F2C fields x80, x81, x82 from s8 to u8.
Adjusted struct CSSData in mn/types.h by combining unk_0x0 and unk_0x1 into u16 unk_0x0, and removed the x190[64] tail padding from CSSDoorsData.
Relaxed HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 in sislib.h to a variadic signature, matching its use as a printf-like text formatting function throughout mnCharSel_8025C020.

Corrected GALE01 symbol map entries around mnCharSel data: resized mnCharSel_803F0DFC from 0x1D0 to 0x190, split subsequent data/string symbols at 803F0F8C through 803F0FC0 and 803F1138 through 803F11A4, and resized mnCharSel_804D5100/804D5108 sdata entries from 0x8 to 0x6.
smart_moves: The match relied on replacing vague cross-module prototypes with concrete return types such as int, u8, u16, and u32; this reduced casts and made the generated code for stat formatting cases line up better.
Using u8* for the gmmain_lib record fields and changing x80/x81/x82 to u8 matched the way those values feed directly into animation-frame selection in inline3.

Splitting data symbols into exact string-sized entries avoided treating trailing strings as part of larger opaque data blobs, which is important for data layout and references around mnCharSel_803F0DFC.
The new mncharsel.static.h isolated bulky static CSS icon/door data from the function work while keeping address-named static data available to the translation unit.
The printf-like varargs declaration for HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 matches actual call patterns such as "%08d", "%09d", "%02d:%02d", Japanese KO text, and NULL clearing calls.
Caching mnCharSel_804D6CB0->match_type into a local in mnCharSel_8025BD30 is a small source change likely made to guide codegen while keeping behavior unchanged.
The language-dependent formatting macro show_kos reduced duplicated Japanese/non-Japanese HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 calls for multiple event-stat cases.
decomp_lessons: For menu code that formats text, accurate printf-style prototypes can be a matching prerequisite; leaving such functions as fixed UNK_T parameters hides important vararg call structure.
When a data object size changes after decompiling adjacent data, update symbols.txt to split exact strings and prevent later data from being swallowed by an oversized object.
Signedness of byte fields matters for codegen even when values are small; record/animation indices used as bytes should be modeled as u8 when the generated loads expect unsigned behavior.
Large static data tables can be moved into a .static.h included only by the C file to make active function matching easier without exporting those data definitions.
Use concrete gm accessor signatures as soon as call sites reveal them; unknown prototypes can force bad argument/return handling in matched menu code.
Struct cleanup can be driven by data-size evidence: CSSData's first two bytes became a u16, and CSSDoorsData's artificial trailing x190[64] was removed after the symbol size was corrected to 0x190.
Language-region branches in Melee menu code often affect both loaded assets (.dat versus .usd) and displayed strings, so matching should preserve those branches explicitly.
naming_conventions: Address-based decomp names were retained for unknown mnCharSel globals and functions, e.g. mnCharSel_8025C020, mnCharSel_803F0A48, mnCharSel_803F0DFC, mnCharSel_804D6CB0.
Lifecycle callback names use descriptive suffixes when known, e.g. mnCharSel_8026688C_OnEnter and mnCharSel_802669F4_OnFrame.
The new file follows the project pattern of a module-local static data header named mncharsel.static.h.
CSS-related structs and data use CSS-prefixed type names such as CSSData, CSSIconsData, CSSDoorsData, and CSSKOStar.
Unknown fields remain offset-named, such as unk_0x0, x80, x81, x82, and selected_since_load, rather than invented semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced PAD_STACK(8) in mnCharSel_8026688C_OnEnter to satisfy stack layout/codegen requirements.
Used static inline helpers rather than out-of-line functions for repeated formatting and animation setup sequences inside mnCharSel_8025C020.
Used exact float literals and macros for icon geometry in the static data table, including comments noting they cannot be enum constants because they are floats.
Used volatile on the static CSSData* mnCharSel_804D6CB0, likely to influence load behavior in matched code; the diff provides evidence of the volatile qualifier but not the author's rationale.
Kept explicit address-style extern declarations for several mnCharSel globals referenced by the newly decompiled function.
Adjusted sdata symbol sizes for mnCharSel_804D5100 and mnCharSel_804D5108 to 0x6, consistent with their use as format/string data pointers in mnCharSel_8025C020.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported +360 bytes of newly matched code: mnCharSel_8026688C_OnEnter improved from 0.00% to 100.00%.
The bot also reported mnCharSel_8025C020 improved by +3928 bytes, from 0.00% to 86.98%, and a small unrelated improvement in main/melee/un/un_2FC9 fn_802FF218 by +10 bytes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish mnCharSel_8025C020 from 86.98% to 100%.
Search for follow-up renames or type refinements for gm_80162BD8 through gm_80163F50 in gm_1601.h.
Check whether mncharsel.static.h data definitions were later moved, renamed, or converted into better typed non-address names.
Inspect later changes to CSSData and CSSDoorsData to confirm the u16 unk_0x0 and removed x190 padding remained stable.
Look for other menu PRs that changed HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 call signatures or used printf-style text formatting to improve matches.
